ocr.text: In Table 1, the analysis of various variables reveals significant findings pertinent to overall survival (OS) and disease-free
survival (DFS) metrics. For venous infiltration (no vs. yes), the hazard ratio (HR) is 2.9 with a 95% confidence interval (CI) of
1.1–7.4 and a p-value of 0.022. In DFS, the HR stands at 1.5 with a 95% CI of 0.8–3.1 and a p-value of 0.176. Edmondson-
Steiner grading (I/II vs.

III/IV) shows an HR of 1.6 with a 95% CI of 0.7–3.7 and a p-value of 0.256 for OS, while DFS demonstrates an HR of 3.0 with
a 95% CI of 1.7–5.3 and a p-value of <0.001. The TNM tumor stage (I/II vs. III/IV) presents an HR of 3.7 and a 95% CI of 1.8–
7.5 with a highly significant p-value of <0.001 for OS, and for DFS, the HR is 4.6 with a 95% CI of 2.5–8.4 and a p-value of
<0.001. Lastly, TPX2 (negative vs. positive) shows an HR of 3.7 with a 95% CI of 1.7–8.1 and a p-value of 0.001 for OS, and

for DFS, the HR is 2.2 with a 95% CI of 1.2–3.9 and a p-value of 0.006.

Table 2 details PCR reaction results, showing for
cytob1, 51 PCR positive animals, 0 PCR negative animals, totaling 73 animals. MAR1bB2 has 0 PCR positive and 16 PCR
negative animals, totaling 73. Bovar2A records 0 PCR positives and 25 negatives, totaling 73. Finally, multiplex PCR reveals
39 PCR positives, 1 negative, and 73 total animals, including 4 T.a + B.b + A.m, 8 T.a + A.m, and 4 B.b + A.m.
